what_it_does: A living guide noticeboard item that directs the capability-workshop to ground its work in the provider-registry archaeology and to treat new provider/agent integration as an extension of the existing capability registry, not a parallel build.
observations: Type is guide, state is living, source is claude_code, author session is ch-3mpkjg3r.
Title frames the item as a Tim-approved link between capability-workshop and provider-registry, grounded in dragnet evidence.
Links three board items: an archaeology charter, a dragnet spec, and the capability-workshop charter.
Body states System A already registers Claude Code and gh-cli as a 2-provider template, so Codex/Workspace-Agents should be added as another provider row.
Mentions a real inventory of 32 roles, 25 decisions, 12 projections, and 8 flows, plus a System B model-catalog to redesign.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, despite the state being marked living.
Uses board:// URI addressing and YAML link kinds references and sourced_from.
standouts: Explicit anti-parallelism instruction: extend the registry, never build a parallel one.
Specific evidence claim that Claude Code + gh-cli form the existing 2-provider template.
Concrete inventory counts (32/25/12/8) cited as the ground for composition.
Title asserts a Tim-approved link, though the body does not record the approval.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard YAML frontmatter schema with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, links, and history.
Uses board:// URIs for internal item references.
States a single clear thesis in the body and supports it with bullet evidence.
Breaks convention by keeping created and updated equal while claiming a living state.
concerns: Inventory counts are asserted without any source or derivation shown in this file.
The Tim-approved claim in the title has no supporting attribution or approval record in the body.
Jargon terms like dragnet evidence and System A/System B are not defined locally.
Created and updated timestamps are identical, so the living state has no visible edit history yet.
notes: A grounding guide that ties the capability-workshop to existing provider-registry archaeology and warns against building a parallel registry.
questions: Who is Tim and where is the approval documented?
Where can the detailed inventory of 32 roles, 25 decisions, 12 projections, and 8 flows be found?
What concrete steps does add a provider row require for Codex and Workspace-Agents?
What is the scope and authority of the System B model-catalog redesign?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to ground the workshop in existing registry archaeology; the file does exactly that by listing evidence and anchors, so purpose and actual align.
intention_signals: It is trying to prevent the capability-workshop from reinventing the provider-registry by anchoring it in already-collected archaeology and framing new agents as additional rows in an existing registry.
novelty: It reframes a workshop charter as an extension of an archaeology-backed registry rather than a standalone planning document, using explicit anti-parallelism language.
contribution: It supplies a concise grounding rationale and a guard against registry duplication when adding new providers/agents, plus pointers to the archaeology charter, dragnet spec, and workshop charter.
summary_for_embedding: A living noticeboard guide asserting that the capability-workshop must build on provider-registry archaeology, extending the existing Claude Code/gh-cli provider registry rather than creating a parallel system, and anchors this claim to three board items.
intention_for_embedding: To align the capability-workshop with existing registry evidence and prevent greenfield duplication when adding providers like Codex and Workspace-Agents.
